content.problemStatement:
You are standing on the coordinate 000 of an integer number line, which extends infinitely in the positive direction.

You will make NNN jumps, each of length either AAA or BBB along the **positive axis**. You can decide whether to make a jump of length AAA or BBB each time, but you must make exactly NNN jumps.

Is it possible for you to end up at coordinate MMM after all NNN jumps?

For example, it is possible to reach M=5M = 5M=5 with N=3N = 3N=3 jumps and A=1,B=2A = 1, B = 2A=1,B=2 by choosing the first jump to be of length A=1A = 1A=1, and the second and third jumps to be of length B=2B = 2B=2. The path you follow is 0→1→3→50 \rightarrow 1 \rightarrow 3 \rightarrow 50→1→3→5.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The only line of input contains 444 integers - N,M,A,BN, M, A, BN,M,A,B, the parameters described in the statement.


Output Format
For each test case, output Yes\text{Yes}Yes or No\text{No}No based on whether it is possible to reach MMM after making NNN jumps.


You may print each character in either case, i.e. yes,YES\text{yes}, \text{YES}yes,YES and yEs\text{yEs}yEs will all be accepted.
constraints: The sum of NNN is **not bounded** over all tests.

sampleTestCases.output:
No
Yes
Yes

No
sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : It is impossible to reach coordinate 222 after 333 jumps. Note that you cannot move backwards, and you must make all NNN jumps.
Test Case 2 : We can take all 333 jumps with length 111.
Test Case 3 : Explained in problem statement.
